identity.canonical_title: LaTeX Source (New Sections) — Copy/Paste into the Paper: S[ρ;t]

theory.canonical_statement: \paragraph{Entropy functional.} Define the total entropy functional \[ \mathcal S[\rho;t] \;=\; -\mathrm{Tr}[\rho\log\rho] \;+\; \frac{1}{T_{\rm env}}\!\int \delta Q_{\rm irr} \;+\; k_B\log\Omega + S_{\rm info}[\mathrm{Wigner}(\psi)], \] subject to the Second Law constraint $\dot{\mathcal S}\ge 0$. In practical terms the entropy budget must include the Von Neumann entropy of the system, Clausius contributions from irreversible heat, a Boltzmann combinatorial term, and the information-theoretic entropy associated with the phase-space (Wigner) distribution of the relevant modes.
theory.technical_interpretation: LaTeX Source (New Sections) — Copy/Paste into the Paper: S[ρ;t] is an integral expression, written mathcal S[ρ;t] = -Tr[ρlogρ] + frac1T_rm env ∫ δ Q_rm irr + k_BlogΩ + S_rm info[W. It is obtained by integrating over a domain, accumulating contributions across the region of interest. It is built from ρ, δ, Ω, ψ, T_ env, Q_ irr, defining mathcal S[ρ;t]. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
